medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The patient states she has been having epigastric pain and nausea for a couple of weeks but it has worsened in the last three days and she has been vomiting. The vomiting has been black and brown in color. She therefore has not been able to eat or drink much of anything recently. Denies fevers or shortness of breath, diarrhea, and has been having small formed bowel movements. ED investigations revealed a pulmonary embolism and gastric outlet obstruction.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Primary diagnosis of metastatic cholangiocarcinoma, prior surgical resection, history of weakness, fatigue, anorexia, and jaundice. Other treatments included gemcitabine and chemoradiation.
medical_history.family_history: Father died of prostate cancer, mother died of dementia, sister has pulmonary hypertension, brother is estranged, uncle has prostate cancer, and aunt has stomach cancer.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital Signs: BP 96/60, HR 86, RR 18, Temp 98.0. General: NAD. Abdomen: Soft, NT, distended, multiple palpable nodules, NG tube in place.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Alert and oriented, no focal deficits. Cardiovascular: RRR, NL S1S2. Pulmonary: Decreased breath sounds diffusely.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Klebsiella pneumoniae >100,000 organisms/mL. Treated with Cipro.

radiographic_examinations.findings: No evidence of deep venous thrombosis in the left lower extremity veins. Small popliteal fossa cyst.
Small to moderate bilateral pleural effusions, similar on the right but new on the left compared to a prior CT.

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: CTA Chest; CT Abdomen & Pelvis with Contrast

findings.Pulmonary: Subsegmental right upper and right middle lobe pulmonary emboli.
findings.Gastric: Stomach and entire thoracic esophagus are newly and abnormally distended and fluid-filled.
findings.Liver: Essentially unchanged lesions related to cholangiocarcinoma.
findings.Omental: Subtle nodularity and peritoneal enhancement suggest metastatic disease.
findings.Ascites: Large volume ascites minimally changed compared to the prior examination.

findings.Liver: 1.7 cm hypoechoic lesion corresponding to hypoattenuating liver lesion seen on recent CT.
findings.Pancreas: Top-normal pancreatic duct measuring up to 3 mm.

other_tests.findings: A malignant intrinsic short stenosis was found in the first part of the duodenum; duodenal stent was placed.

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Gastric Outlet Obstruction due to Malignant Intrinsic Short Stenosis in D1 secondary to Metastatic Cholangiocarcinoma
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with epigastric pain and vomiting, with imaging revealing a gastric outlet obstruction. An endoscopy found malignant intrinsic short stenosis in the first part of the duodenum, confirming the obstruction's cause. Additionally, the patient was also found to have a pulmonary embolism during the course of evaluation.
